channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Estonia steps up patrols in the icy Baltic Sea in a show of force after suspected cable sabotage
item.description: ABOARD THE EML SAKALA IN THE BALTIC SEA (AP) — As they plied the gray, icy waters of the Baltic Sea west of Russia on Thursday, the crew of the Estonian minehunter EML Sakala kept a careful eye on any vessels slowing down suspiciously or suddenly changing course.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS and HENDRIK OSULA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Firefighters hope for a break from fierce winds fueling devastating blazes in Los Angeles area
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Firefighters hoped for a break Friday from fierce winds fueling massive blazes in the Los Angeles area that have killed 10 people, obliterated whole neighborhoods and set the nation's second-largest city on edge.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Venezuela latest: Nicolás Maduro takes office as global backlash and sanctions mount
item.description: CARACAS, Venezuela (AP) — Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro was sworn in for a third six-year term Friday despite international condemnation of his recent reelection as illegitimate, as his administration grows increasingly brazen in cracking down on opponents.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge extends Megan Thee Stallion’s protection order against rapper Tory Lanez until 2030
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Megan Thee Stallion has been granted a restraining order from imprisoned rapper Tory Lanez until early 2030.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump is sentenced in his hush money case, but the judge declines to impose any punishment
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump was sentenced Friday in his hush money case, but the judge declined to impose any punishment, an outcome that cements his conviction while freeing him to return to the White House unencumbered by the threat of a jail term or a fine.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, JENNIFER PELTZ, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge sentences Trump in hush money case but declines to impose any punishment
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump was sentenced Friday in his hush money case, but the judge declined to impose any punishment, an outcome that cements his conviction but frees him to return to the White House unencumbered by the threat of a jail term or a fine.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, KTLA 5

item.title: California wildfires: Essential phone numbers for Los Angeles-area residents and how you can help them
item.description: Emergency and useful phone numbers to have if you are a Los Angeles-area resident, as well as additional information on how to help those impacted by the wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Live: Supreme Court hears arguments over planned TikTok ban
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The law that could ban TikTok is coming before the Supreme Court on Friday, with the justices largely holding the app's fate in their hands. Cameras are not allowed in the court, but you can hear audio of the debate in the video player above.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Biden administration extends temporary status for 800,000 from Venezuela and El Salvador
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security says about 600,000 Venezuelans and more than 200,000 El Salvadorans already living in the United States can legally remain another 18 months.
item.dc:creator: Gisela Salomon, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Civilian drone hits, damages firefighting aircraft over Palisades Fire
item.description: A firefighting aircraft that was operating over the Palisades Fire is grounded and out of service after it was struck and damaged by a civilian’s drone on Thursday afternoon, officials confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge sentences Trump in hush money case but declines to impose any punishment
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump was sentenced Friday in his hush money case, but the judge declined to impose any punishment, an outcome that cements his conviction but frees him to return to the White House unencumbered by the threat of a jail term or a fine.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, Fox 5

item.title: Jamie Lee Curtis donates $1M to LA wildfire relief efforts
item.description: Jamie Lee Curtis says her family is donating $1 million to relief efforts amid the wildfires in California that have devastated the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: 2 dead, over 5,300 structures destroyed as Palisades Fire continues growing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (KTLA) - Officials confirmed two people were killed and over 5,316 structures have been damaged or destroyed as fire crews struggle to gain containment on the over 19,000-acre Palisades Fire: the most destructive wildfire in Los Angeles history.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, NewsNation

item.title: What to know about thousands of evacuations and homes burned in Los Angeles-area fires
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — At least 10 people were killed and thousands of structures were burned as fierce wildfires raged in the Los Angeles area, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What ignited the deadly California wildfires? Investigators consider an array of possibilities
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Investigators are considering an array of possible ignition sources for the huge fires that have killed at least 10 people and destroyed thousands of homes and businesses in the Los Angeles area.
item.dc:creator: JASON DEAREN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Delta Air Lines says 4 passengers injured after flight was aborted at Atlanta airport
item.description: A powerful winter storm that dumped heavy snow and glazed roads with ice across much of Texas and Oklahoma has lumbered eastward into the Deep South and elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Stengle And Kendria Lafleur, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Google Trends: Nearly every US state is searching the Broncos over the Bills
item.description: We looked at Google Trends statistics to see whether the Broncos or the Bills were the talk of the country – and one team won by a landslide.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, Fox 5

item.title: New LA-area fire prompts more evacuations while over 10,000 structures lost to the 2 biggest blazes
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The two biggest wildfires ravaging the Los Angeles area have burned at least 10,000 homes, buildings and other structures, officials said Thursday as they urged more people to heed evacuation orders after a new blaze ignited and quickly grew.
item.dc:creator: JAIMIE DING, JULIE WATSON and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Los Angeles landmarks from film, TV damaged by wildfires
item.description: The high school where Brian De Palma brought Stephen King’s “Carrie” to life, Will Rogers’ ranch house, and a motel owned by William Randolph Hearst are among some of the famous structures that have been damaged or destroyed by the California wildfires.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Anita Bryant, anti-gay rights activist and singer, dead at 84
item.description: Anita Bryant Dry, known popularly as Anita Bryant — a singer and crusader against the progress of gay rights — has died at the age of 84, her family announced in an obituary.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Supreme Court rejects Trump’s bid to delay sentencing in his New York hush money case
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A sharply divided Supreme Court on Thursday rejected President-elect Donald Trump’s final bid to put his New York hush-money case on hold, clearing the way for him to be sentenced for felony crimes days before he returns to the presidency. The court’s 5-4 order allows Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, MICHAEL HILL and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: In Los Angeles, people snap up air filters and wear masks against smoke pollution
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — With levels of sooty air pollution far too high across swaths of the Los Angeles area, Dana Benton is sleeping with a mask on, even with her air purifier running.
item.dc:creator: DORANY PINEDA and TAMMY WEBBER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Apalachee High School student arrested for allegedly bringing gun to campus months after deadly mass shooting
item.description: Just four months after a devastating mass shooting at Georgia's Apalachee High School, another 14-year-old student brought a gun to campus, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: These products should be kept in your car to handle icy roads
item.description: With winter firmly in place, so is the season of dangerous road conditions. Many items can make it safer to travel.
item.dc:creator: Jordan C. Woika, ABC 4

item.title: LA Mayor Bass stonewalls reporter for 90 seconds at airport
item.description: Video taken on an airport jetway shows Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass repeatedly ignoring questions from a reporter about her response to the wildfires ripping through the area this week.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: 4 in 10 companies planning job cuts due to AI: Survey
item.description: Companies across the U.S., at least four in 10, are likely to cut jobs as artificial intelligence (AI) continues to advance, new research shows.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Aerial video shows before and after views of Palisades Fire in Los Angeles
item.description: “I couldn’t image what we were preparing to see once we got over here on this side of the hill,” pilot Rich Prickett said.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, NewsNation

item.title: Michigan woman gets jail after boy brought pink gun to school
item.description: A woman who illegally possessed a pink handgun that a child took to a Grand Rapids elementary school in a backpack has been sentenced to a year in jail.
item.dc:creator: John Hogan, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Photo gallery: Aerial photos show the scope of destruction from the Pacific Palisades wildfire
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Street after street, there is little but dark ash and ruin where a wealthy seaside enclave favored by celebrities once stood. Blackened trees stand out amid the rubble, and the occasional mansion remains, suddenly exposed to view from all sides.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watchdog groups seeks investigation into RFK Jr. for voting from a 'sham' address
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (AP) — New York election officials are being urged to investigate whether Robert F. Kennedy Jr.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Lisa Kudrow says Matthew Perry left a note for her inside a recognizable 'Friends' prop
item.description: "I hadn't opened it up or looked inside it, but yeah he did. He had a note in there and I forgot about it," Kudrow said.
item.dc:creator: Emily Smith, KRON4

item.title: Officer Michael Horan honored with funeral after being killed in the line of duty
item.description: GREENSBORO, N.C. (WGHP) — The community came together to celebrate the life of a Greensboro police officer, just over two weeks after he was killed in the line of duty. Officer Michael Horan's funeral was held at Westover Church in Greensboro.
item.dc:creator: Emily Mikkelsen, NewsNation

item.title: Lawsuit by New Orleans truck attack victims says city, contractors failed to implement safety system
item.description: Six people who were injured and the father of a man who was killed in the New Year's truck attack filed a lawsuit Thursday against the City of New Orleans and two contractors, claiming they failed to protect revelers from an Army veteran who sped around a police blockade and raced down Bourbon Stree
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Jean Smart calls on TV networks to not air award shows, instead donate to Los Angeles wildfire relief
item.description: Jean Smart is urging TV networks to refrain from airing glitzy awards shows amid the devastating wildfires in the Los Angeles area, and to donate money to relief efforts instead.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Los Angeles wildfires leave hundreds of thousands without power
item.description: An outage map provided by the Los Angeles Department of Water and Power (LADWP) showed just how widespread the outages extended
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, NewsNation

item.title: Eaton Fire victim dies holding a hose, defending home
item.description: According to his loved ones, 66-year-old Victor Shaw died trying to defend the home that had been in his family for nearly 55 years.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, NewsNation

item.title: Water crisis in Central Virginia leaves residents and businesses struggling
item.description: Hanover County remains under a boil water advisory, with no clear timeline from officials on when the situation will return to normal.
item.dc:creator: Sahara Sriraman, NewsNation
